commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the external PR has a concrete classifier/security defect and lacks contributor-supplied real reset proof.; Needs attention: The patch can route sensitive trajectory runtime archives through transcript-oriented discovery because the new archive filename matches current usage-counted transcript classifiers.; Review finding: [P1] Exclude trajectory archives from transcript disc... (sha=17e63aec7f6f54dc82bc111058e909cbfac40272)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds reset-only archiving of trajectory runtime and pointer sidecars in `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` and adds regression tests for reset versus delete behavior.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +63. Total +97 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: PR head creates reset-suffixed trajectory archive names, while current classifiers accept broad reset/deleted suffixes as usage-counted transcripts and usage discovery parses the stem. I did not run a live reset path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the external PR has a concrete classifier/security defect and lacks contributor-supplied real reset proof.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve trajectory on reset for outbound forensics (fixes #90707)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #90707.

On `/new` and `/reset`, OpenClaw archives the session transcript as
`<sid>.jsonl.reset.<ts>` but the sibling **trajectory** file
`<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` (the assistant's tool calls and tool results) and its
`<sid>.trajectory-path.json` pointer are lost — overwritten when the session
file path is reused, or left dangling and later reclaimed as an orphan. For
sessions that issued outbound communications (WhatsApp/email/SMS/Telegram),
this makes post-incident forensics impossible: after a wrong send there is no
record of the exact tool args or the tool result that preceded the send.

This change mirrors the transcript-archive behavior for the trajectory. In the
shared reset path `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` (used by **both** reset
entry points — the auto-reply session rollover in
`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts` and the gateway reset service in
`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`), when `reason === "reset"` the
trajectory runtime file and its pointer are renamed to `.reset.<ts>` archives
instead of being left to be deleted.

### Why this shape

- **Single owner.** Both reset flows funnel through
  `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed`, so one gated branch covers both rather
  than patching each call site.
- **No new retention code / no unbounded growth.** The reset retention sweep
  (`cleanupArchivedSessionTranscripts` →
  `parseSessionArchiveTimestamp(name, "reset")`) keys on the generic
  `.reset.<ts>` suffix, so `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl.reset.<ts>` and
  `<sid>.trajectory-path.json.reset.<ts>` are pruned by the existing
  `resetArchiveRetentionMs` rule automatically.
- **No re-deletion.** `isTrajectoryRuntimeArtifactName` requires an exact
  `.trajectory.jsonl` ending, so the archived form is no longer treated as a
  live trajectory and the existing `removeSessionTrajectoryArtifacts` cleanup
  leaves it alone.
- **Deleted/pruned sessions unchanged.** The branch is gated on
  `reason === "reset"`; `reason === "deleted"` still hands the trajectory to the
  existing removal path (covered by a regression test).

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.archive-events.test.ts` —
  28 passed, including two new cases (reset preserves trajectory + pointer;
  deleted does not archive them).
- `pnpm test src/trajectory/cleanup.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.archive-cleanup.test.ts` — all pass (no prune/cleanup regressions).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — exit 0.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>` and `oxfmt --check` — clean.
- `pnpm check:import-cycles` — 0 runtime value cycles (new `../trajectory/paths.js` import introduces none).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Session reset (`/new`, `/reset`) deleted the sibling
  `trajectory.jsonl` + pointer while preserving the transcript; they are now
  archived as `.reset.<ts>` alongside the transcript.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux source checkout (Node 22), Vitest +
  tsgo + oxlint + oxfmt.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  `pnpm test src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.archive-events.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** New test "preserves the sibling trajectory and pointer
  on reset" asserts `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` and `<sid>.trajectory-path.json`
  are gone from their live names and present as `*.reset.<ts>` archives; the
  companion test asserts a `deleted` reason leaves the live siblings untouched.
- **Observed result after fix:** Test Files 4 passed, Tests 28 passed.
- **What was not tested:** A full live gateway reset round-trip and the
  `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_DIR` override placement (intentionally out of scope —
  the fix targets the default sibling placement described in the issue).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
